response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial and GIS experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated distributed systems thinking from his Sociomantic work, but the core requirement for the role is modern C++ or Rust development and he has neither in any recent capacity. His C++ exposure is limited to a 2007–2008 role at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project nearly two decades ago. His primary commercial stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which does not align with the C++-heavy, systems-level routing pipeline work this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial and GIS platform development spanning 15+ years through GISCollective and Green Map projects
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O)
Broad full-stack and multi-language experience signals strong adaptability and willingness to learn new technical domains
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; last meaningful C++ work was in 2007–2008 at National Instruments, disqualifying for the primary technical requirement
Geospatial work is event-driven data collection and mapping, not routing tile generation or navigation pipeline optimization
No evidence of OS-level or distributed-systems work in the systems programming sense (fault tolerance, consistency protocols, service discovery) expected at this seniority
